SPEC AMENDMENT to freeze-at-stamp (maintainer clarification 2026-07-12 evening, amends the work_opened row's design point 2): the frozen tracker copy must be COMPLETELY ISOLATED at the DATABASE level, not a sibling schema pair inside the live toy db. Reason, his: if read-access tracking into the db is ever instrumented, experiment/probe reads against a same-database copy would contaminate the live db's read-access record regardless of information freshness -- isolation is about the OBSERVATION SURFACE, not only the data. Implementation: CREATE DATABASE frozen_<shortcommit> (or --db-name override) on the same host; pg_dump the source schema pair and restore into it with the id-cutoff truncation; grants/read-only posture unchanged. Honest residual, named not hidden: same-CLUSTER surfaces remain shared (the global log stream, pg_stat_activity) with entries distinguishable by database name -- a future read tracker filters by database or sets per-database logging; full cluster separation is a host-level act and per the standing no-perimeter ruling is not proposed here. Refusals gain: database name already exists -> refuse. Teardown becomes DROP DATABASE + rm dest. RCA SYNTHESIS: what-did-we-miss (orchestrator, 2026-07-12 evening; Track 1 blind + Track 2 informed, both complete). HEADLINE, unsoftened: THE BLIND SWEEP DID NOT SURFACE READ-ACCESS LOGGING. The maintainer's pre-registered trigger (miss it again -> shelving-grade signal about the method) FIRES on its own terms. WHY IT MISSED -- and this is the load-bearing finding: the blind auditor executed its method faithfully (enumerate the standards the project itself invokes, walk each standard's own family structure inward -- it produced six genuine, new ABSENT-AND-UNNAMED findings doing so) but NIST never made its invoked-standards list, because NIST IS A WORD IN THIS PROJECT, NOT A SOURCE: the operative standard (law/briefs/safety-critical-logging/BRIEF.md) grounds in ~25 external standards and cites NIST zero times (Track 2, grepped); NIST appears in the corpus only as a LENS NAME (regulator assessment) and as conversational shorthand -- never as a cited document with a family structure attached. Every downstream layer then inherited the omission faithfully: the brief (authored outward from an admired-standards set), the conformance map (additive, never rows the full register; G12 never mentioned), conformance_check.py (verifies claimed scope only -- honest about it; the clean verdicts were true answers to unasked questions), the four/five-lens panels (NIST lens pointed at OUR docs, not NIST's catalog), and now the blind audit (project-rooted standards discovery cannot see a standard the corpus never operatively cites). SAME ROOT CAUSE, FIFTH INSTANCE. Track 2's additional finding, the homonym trap, explains the maintainer's raised-twice-and-died recollection: 'read logging' EXISTS as CAPABILITIES item 27 (read_observer, I6 reviewer-independence evidence) -- a real mechanism under the colliding name answered the question each time it surfaced; the NIST-AU sense (who reads the system of record) was never the question item 27 answers. WHAT THE BLIND SWEEP DID PROVE: within its input list the method works -- six new ranked ABSENT-AND-UNNAMED findings (RG 1.170 test-artifact separation, silent in every doc incl. the same-day self-audit; Part 11 authority-vs-identity gap 11.10(g); DO-178C A-9 independent-SQA function; harness's own structural coverage; GSN-dialectic/ASP-defeater twin work never connected; managerial/financial independence values the schema accepts but cannot ground). THE CHARACTERIZED FIX (single point): the invoked-standards list must be a MAINTAINER-RATIFIED EXPLICIT REGISTRY (including standards he holds the project to that no document yet cites -- NIST SP 800-53 first), and every completeness exercise runs against THAT registry, never against corpus-discovered citations; plus Track 2's four method fixes (subtractive conformance-map pass; external-catalog-as-required-reading for lens commissions; homonym disclosure rule; standing family-completeness commission). TESTABLE: re-run the blind audit with the ratified registry -- 800-53's AU family surfaces read-auditing by construction or the method is genuinely broken. THE DECISION IS THE MAINTAINER'S, prepared per his own clause: (a) shelve, or (b) adopt the registry fix + method fixes and re-test. Orchestrator's one recommendation, honestly argued: (b) -- the failure is now a characterized single-point omission (standards-as-words) reproduced consistently across five independent layers, not an open-ended method failure; the blind audit demonstrated the machinery finds silences wherever its input list reaches; the fix is small, and its success criterion is mechanical. But the trigger fired as he defined it, and (a) is a legitimate reading of that fact.

CLARIFICATION from the maintainer (2026-07-13, supersedes the question/answer framing inside the typed-table-constructor-experiment work_opened row and refines the broadcast-test description in the incident row): the question-answer reading of column/row was an APPROXIMATION -- his one-off reading of that particular table. The general statement, type-theoretically: a table is a STRUCTURED PRODUCT TYPE (each row a tuple typed by the column headers; the label column's header is a type former and every row label must inhabit it), and the defect class is AN ILL-TYPED ELEMENT IN A STRUCTURED PRODUCT. The broadcast test is a human-readable type-check (distribute the former over a claimed inhabitant, judge well-formedness) -- one witness procedure for ill-typing, not the definition. Consequence for typed-table-constructor-experiment: the constructor enforces rows-inhabit-the-declared-type for WHATEVER type the author declares, never a mandatory question/answer schema; this is the intake-grammar discipline (typed refusal at the write boundary) applied to tables.

COMMISSION CORRECTION watchdog-liveness-harness (maintainer, 2026-07-13, supersedes the framing in the work_opened row): this is NOT a never-police-invariant matter -- that guard was the orchestrator's category error (policing punishes cost overruns; this detects stalled execution). It is a SAFETY FEATURE against unattended zero-progress: his scenario verbatim in substance -- he leaves the terminal expecting a workflow done, returns 2 hours later, it has been stuck in a Claude Code python command bug since minute three: two hours of wasted effort, an OPERATIONAL HAZARD, especially in a medical setting. Design-bar consequences: (1) RECOVERY is the point, not observation -- a journal entry an absent operator cannot read discharges nothing; the design must reach for kill-and-retry of the hung unit (re-dispatch the agent, resume the workflow from its journal), timeout-tightening at dispatch time from expected-duration data, and only then surfacing; (2) where recovery is genuinely impossible from hook/harness position, say so honestly AND say what the next-best is (e.g. the stall is at least converted to a typed failure the workflow's own control flow can route around, instead of an indefinite hang); (3) treat as a safety mechanism at the fail-safe design bar, not ergonomics; (4) the estimate-consumption and configurable-slack mechanics from the original row stand unchanged.

CORRECTION (maintainer, 2026-07-14): row 372's ent-observatory evidence-surface restriction ('the ACTION-STREAM surfaces only, never the session transcript... transcripts stay private') overreads its cited source. Row 296's actual text grades ~/.claude internals/session transcripts/token accounting as DIAGNOSTIC-GRADE, never guarantee surfaces -- a statement about what the HARNESS can promise a DOWNSTREAM DEPLOYMENT OPERATOR (product-facing guarantee scope), not a restriction on the autoharn team's own reads of its own test deployments' transcripts for development/dogfooding purposes (maintainer's own words: 'action-stream-is-evidentiary-basis is very much true for downstream USERS... WE develop it and use the transcripts to determine what we are missing out on'). CLARIFIED SCOPE, going forward: action-stream-is-evidentiary-basis bounds what guarantees autoharn-as-product makes to an operator; it does NOT bound autoharn-the-team's own observability into ent or any other dogfood deployment. The ent-observatory work item's (row 372) 'never the session transcript' clause is SUPERSEDED by this clarification -- transcript reads by the autoharn team, for autoharn's own learning, are in-scope evidence, still read-only against the deployment's own files (that hard constraint is untouched and unrelated to this correction). Provenance note: row 372's model-authorship is unprovable (no model-attribution column yet, tracker item model-attribution-tracking); this correction does not assert who wrote the overreach, only that it was one.

ORCHESTRATOR POLICY (maintainer, 2026-07-14): standing autoharn-project rules/feedback stop being recorded in Claude Code's out-of-band memory system (~/.claude/projects/.../memory/) -- maintainer's reasoning: session memories are ephemeral, accumulate without supersession, and poison context; the ledger is BUILT for defeasible reasoning (supersedes/amends, review verdicts, explicit staleness) which plain markdown is not. Going forward: durable autoharn-specific rules/corrections are ledgered via ./led decision, never written to the memory system. Four memories written earlier this session are being migrated into ledger rows now (this row + the three below) and their files removed.

MIGRATED FROM MEMORY (orch-handoff-quarantined): do not open/diff/quote ORCH-HANDOFF.md -- two Fable session-load rejections were caused by attempting to load a session on it; a staged uncommitted edit removing its ~/ent picom/C-GLSL description was the maintainer's own scrub of the suspected trigger line. Hydrate via ./pickup + maintainer-provided snippets instead; if a git operation is blocked by its staged edit, stash/pop that path without inspecting it.

MIGRATED FROM MEMORY (relayed-transcripts-vent-vs-commission): when the maintainer relays a downstream session transcript, check whether he's commissioning autoharn work or venting about that session's conduct before dispatching -- 2026-07-14 specimen: a quoted ent-session transcript read as a commission and got dispatched as one; he clarified he meant the target session should have done it, was venting about its laziness, but accepted the build since it was generically useful. Ask or state the scoping assumption explicitly before dispatching on a relayed transcript.

MIGRATED FROM MEMORY (hold-instructions-need-resume-triggers): a 'wait until X is resolved' instruction needs an explicit resume trigger on both sides or resumption stalls on human memory -- 2026-07-14 specimen, self-diagnosed by the maintainer via ADR-0000 Rule 2(b) (the question aimed at the executive: what operational lapse -- an unmechanized, review-only discipline -- let this recur). When holding pending a condition, treat the condition's resolution as its own resume trigger; when issuing a hold, state the resume condition explicitly.

MIGRATED FROM MEMORY (report-only-means-no-write-tools): zero tolerance for any write/edit attempt from an agent whose mandate is report/observe, additive or not -- kill-on-sight is correct, not overreaction. 2026-07-14 specimen: an ent-observation dispatch was scoped to also adjudicate ADR-0013 and act on its own verdict by drafting into design/; the maintainer killed it the instant it reached for Edit, and confirmed afterward this was correct given his standing 'never signed off on destructive side effects' rule -- additive-vs-destructive isn't his discriminator, authorized-vs-not is. A report/observe mandate gets no write tools reachable, full stop; investigate-and-act is a separate, explicit license.

phil consult DELIVERED af463a8 (design/PHIL-DEFEASIBLE-DECISIONS-AND-HYDRATED-DOCS.md, 569 lines incl. web-verified prior-art section per the maintainer's mid-flight addition). Verdicts: idea-1 Claim A (formal defeasible store of rulings) is already this project; Claim B strong form (act on projected rulings) REJECTED -- authority does not interpolate, rulings are performatives, the self-escalation incident is the specimen read harder (right answer + wrong party = still the defect); tenable form = deontic classifier over EXPLICIT delegations with the permit/refuse asymmetry as the bright line (inferred decisions may refuse/defer/classify, never permit -- default-deny). Idea-2: right diagnosis, hard part assumed solved -- staleness is CONSERVED unless fact-writes are verb-coupled; hydrating from a discipline-maintained store relocates the rot; prior-art graveyard (SFS/WinFS = hand-authored metadata) vs survivors (/proc, Plan 9 = mechanism-written facts) empirically confirms the verb-coupling criterion. Composition bright line: hydration preserves epistemic type -- an inferred decision never renders as current truth. Novel contributions: preference-act vs assertion-act split (recency-priority right for preferences, wrong for memory-claims -- dissolves the confabulation worry without modeling the man); FUSE's strongest justification is the READ-WITNESS (AU read-access logging that write-side hooks structurally cannot provide); explicit rulings are the one already-verb-coupled fact family, hence the natural FIRST hydration family.

PARKED (maintainer, 2026-07-15, explicitly not scheduled until he has tested the panel): panel v-next idea, banked verbatim -- 'wondering if it is possible to have the harness enforce a kind of "context scope" that will literally force the output of Claude to contain the specific ledger items (if any) that are of concern; or, perhaps, use a soft version (unenforced protocol with robust parsing in the SPA). What I would like for that, is then that you have two kinds of views, a "browsing view" and a "tandem view", the latter hydrating from turn completion events and you have rich texts with links that you can hover with a synopsis of the corresponding ledger item (clicking it would take you to some kind of structured relational view, same as in browse mode).' Orchestrator note for the eventual builder: the hard-enforce variant has an existing harness home -- the Stop-hook layer already classifies final turn output (demurral detector precedent), so a turn-output-must-cite-its-ledger-rows gate is the same shape; the soft protocol + SPA parsing composes as the cheap first step and the tandem view's hydration event is exactly the Stop hook's firing.

PARKED IDEA banked (maintainer, 2026-07-15 ~07:30, his words: 'hearkning back to defeasible-FUSE, if we store pg_hba.conf on that, we could notionally manage the hba rigamarole in a consistent manner'): pg_hba.conf as a DERIVED artifact from the verified knowledge store -- deployments declare access facts (role, db, source CIDR, auth method, priority), the deductive engine derives the single canonical ordered file, FUSE materializes it at the path postgres reads. What makes this more than whimsy: pg_hba is first-match-wins total order, the artifact class hand-editing handles worst (shadowing) and ASP handles natively -- derivation gives no-shadowing proofs, every-grant-reachable proofs, and no-underivable-line guarantees; defeasible broad-allow/specific-deny is the logic's home idiom; the config-fragments-need-the-real-file ruling dissolves for any artifact class that becomes fully derived; bootstrap/provision-db.sh (built this morning) becomes a fact-emitter rather than a line-generator, a natural migration path. Composes with the philosopher consult (design/PHIL-DEFEASIBLE-DECISIONS-AND-HYDRATED-DOCS.md) and the parked fuse-vfs-knowledge-hydration item; this row adds the first CONCRETE artifact candidate with an operator pain it provably removes. SCOPING CLARIFICATION for item-bracketed-work-discipline and ledger-kind-resource-semantics (maintainer, 2026-07-15, near-verbatim: 'our ledger should be git-agnostic. Git just happens to be ubiquitous. The question at hand has a generic flavor... independent of the toolset'): the invariant pair is generic over effect stores -- (1) every externally-visible effect attributable to an open resource, (2) close requires the item's DECLARED EFFECT-SCOPE SETTLED. Git-tree-clean-in-scope is one instantiation; a schema migration settled, a remote publication verified, a file tree materialized are others. Layering consequence, binding on the eventual builder: the kernel/grammar layer knows only declared scopes and settlement witnesses (store-agnostic vocabulary); per-store adapters (git working tree today, others later) live at the verb/boundary layer and are the only components that can answer is-this-scope-settled. No git vocabulary enters the kernel. This keeps the discipline portable to deployments whose effect stores are not git at all -- consistent with ADR-0003-family portability posture.

Maintainer ruling 2026-07-15: ONE branch, full stop. Worktrees fine; branches are the transaction-paradigm violation witnessed today (v1.1 cut-only README/USER-CONFIGURATION edits authored in the cut worktree, never backported to next, silently re-deleted by the v1.1.2 cut from next). Content is authored on the single working branch ONLY; public main is a pure derivation (a cut is a projection, ADR-0012 derive-don-t-duplicate); an edit inside a cut is the defect to make unrepresentable. Recovery: Sonnet damage scout first, then the mechanization.

Maintainer ratification 2026-07-15: typed-table constructor ADOPTED for NEW load-bearing tables (capability matrices, audit dispositions -- the class where a wrong row is a wrong claim), conditional on the regeneration gate that closes the experiment-named two-home hazard (constructor call is the single home; committed markdown provably regenerated from it, byte-drift refused -- same pure-derivation pattern as public cuts). NOT mandated corpus-wide: existing correct tables stay under the shape gate + report-only detector net. RATIONALE (maintainer, verbatim core): forced articulation works because a type violation is itself a violation of English predication, which LLMs are overwhelmingly unlikely to produce -- serializing the row-label claim into subject-copula-predicate moves it into the distribution where the model competence lives. Fable boundary noted on the same record: this nearly eliminates INATTENTIVE ill-typing (the row-293 class, header constraint decayed from attention mid-table) but not MOTIVATED ill-typing (fluent coercion, cf. the same-day time-budget rationalization specimen); for the motivated case the value is that a false articulation is an on-record quotable claim the detector net and A:B:C can contest, where an unstated one was nothing. Build of the regeneration gate to be itemized separately.

STANDING DISCIPLINE (maintainer ruling 2026-07-16, verbatim): 'for all large-scale changes we'll want an independent review of the changes as a standing discipline (with a particular eye towards ADR-0000 -- the reason is that, at least for humans, when we struggle, we tend to wear out and default to the first solution we see; I suspect LLMs also have that mental fog under circumstantial duress habit).' Q3 shape chosen (orchestrator, under the 2026-07-17 delegation; rationale on the record per self-application rule): a 'led briefing' verb as the single source of truth for the reactive-discovery rules text, plus a thin SessionStart hook (startup|compact|resume) that shells to the world's own ./led briefing -- NOT a hand-maintained static doc (would drift, ADR-0012 duplicated-truth) and NOT prompt-side convention only (does not survive fresh sessions). Forward-looking properties per the maintainer's guideline: (1) content rides the led template, so every adopting world gets the briefing versioned with the verbs it describes; (2) apparatus.json mechanisms.rules_briefing.extra_items lets a serious deployment append world-specific rules without forking the template; (3) subagent coverage is by composition, not new machinery: orchestrator briefs include the verb's output or instruct running it first. Non-invasive: no kernel, no law, additive hook + additive verb; replaceable later if a better shape emerges (maintainer's own stated tolerance). Maintainer proposal 2026-07-17, verbatim: 'By the way, I am looking at my notes and am obliged to ask about our demurral detector (though, with autoharn in place that is almost a non-problem these days): certain phrases like overkill are pretty good static detectors that, if you really wanted to, it could instruct the agent using that term to please be mindful of ADR-0013 and reconsider or something. Of course, that instruction would trigger even on false positives, but framing it in that way is sort of benign, I think -- let the agent itself disregard it if the agent thinks it is a false positive.' Orchestrator assessment on the record: the hook's existing anti-blocklist stance (docstring, ADR-0011 Rule 4 paraphrase-engine argument) targets blocklists as sole/enforcing detectors; it does not bar a zero-cost, observer-only static tier whose false positives cost one disregardable sentence -- especially since the LLM classifier tier is costed and default OFF, i.e. most worlds currently run NO detection. Design: static tier always-on-able at the same attachment points, nudge text names the hard negatives as legitimate disregards, classifier verdict governs when on, docstring amended to disclose the enumeration-fails-open weakness honestly. Fail-safe additive; observer posture and the maintainer-gated promotion rule untouched.

Refinement, maintainer 2026-07-17 verbatim: 'I think the detector is either off, or it is claude -p which costs money or API tokens (same thing, really). I am saying there can be a third option.' Spec for demurral-static-tier accordingly: a third MODE VALUE in mechanisms.demurral_detect.mode -- off (default, unchanged) | static (NEW: free phrase-tier nudges only, zero model calls) | observe (costed classifier as today, static tier as its fallback on classifier hiccup). This hook's own _resolve_mode copy widens its vocabulary locally; no other hook's mode vocabulary touched; unrecognized values still fall back to off (never-widen preserved).

Adding a third free 'static' mode (zero-cost phrase-tier fallback/first-tier) to mechanisms.demurral_detect per maintainer proposal 2026-07-17: off unchanged, static (new, no model call ever, matches ADR-0013 Rule 3's canonical demurral vocabulary), observe (unchanged classifier, falls back to static tier when classifier errors/times out). Docstring's anti-blocklist paragraph amended to preserve the argument for the enforcing/sole case while disclosing the static tier as a deliberately weak, observer-only first tier.

Refinement, maintainer 2026-07-17 verbatim: 'The detection corpus should be user-editable and exists in a well-documented location so they dont have to go in and do surgery.' Maintainer question + orchestrator recommendation, 2026-07-18, verbatim maintainer words: 'What do you suggest on (e)? It is something I obviously will want.' Recommendation given: two families exist for surviving the enclosing rollback (a refusal's RAISE EXCEPTION aborts the whole transaction, so the meta-event cannot be written inside it -- SAVEPOINT does not help, it is wiped too) -- (A) kernel-side dblink-loopback autonomous transaction, complete (catches raw-psql bypasses) but introduces an external-dependency failure surface INSIDE the trigger that could itself fail silently, exactly the disease this arc cures; (B) CLI-side fresh-connection logging in led.tmpl's existing error handler, zero new kernel machinery, fails LOUD if the second write itself fails, and accepts the SAME direct-psql trust boundary section 8 already discloses -- backed empirically by this session's own spy evidence (the panel's one raw-SQL temptation was refused before execution, by norm). Recommended: B as v1 shape, A named as the escalation path if raw-write bypass becomes a real problem. Written into the spec's reserved decision (e) as its stated default, per the D-6 precedent (spec states a default, maintainer's call stands reserved).

Maintainer pushback, verbatim: 'I disapprove of B on the principle that a bad actor could easily circumvent it. Am I missing something? On the other hand, I am not sure what a dblink loopback even refers to.' CORRECTION, self-filed: the pushback is correct, not a misunderstanding -- the orchestrator conflated two distinct trust boundaries. The REFUSAL itself is kernel/trigger-enforced and fires identically for led-issued or raw-psql writes; only the RECORDING of a refusal was CLI-dependent under option B, so a bad actor who simply avoids the CLI -- the first move of anyone probing a governed system -- gets refused but leaves zero trace, which is strictly worse than an honest user specifically on the probing-detection axis (e) exists for. This is a different threat model than section 8's already-disclosed direct-psql-writer trust boundary (dishonest-but-attempted-through-governance data), not the same one. REVISED recommendation: option A (dblink loopback autonomous transaction), kernel-level, tool-agnostic, with a hardening requirement that closes the orchestrator's own original objection to A: the trigger must catch its OWN dblink failure and re-raise the original refusal with the log failure appended to the message, so loud-on-failure holds without CLI cooperation. B dropped entirely, including as a redundant addition (would duplicate the fact, violating the same single-home discipline this arc has been enforcing all day).

Self-filed hazard-in-reach flag: reserved decision (e)'s dblink-loopback default (ledger row 1413) is independently refuted by design/judgment/engine/engine-panel/refute-architecture.md flaw 1 (a prior project generation, same refuter model class, near-identical mechanism: a refusal journal populated via dblink/postgres_fdw loopback from inside a BEFORE INSERT trigger) -- 'undeclared new dependency, a connection per refusal on the subject's latency-budgeted path, and needs its own failure semantics (journal write fails -> refuse anyway? then completeness is gone again)'. This is CLAUDE.md's hazard-in-reach standard: judgment/ is archived-unless-cited, but a hazard found while touching adjacent work does not get routed around for that reason. Combined with today's fresh reviewer independently re-deriving the same three objections plus a fourth (resource exhaustion), and my own 'no silent-failure surface either' claim being an overclaim (double failure degrades to loud-but-incomplete, not to full closure), (e) is REOPENED rather than re-decided a third time unilaterally. Option B (CLI-side) remains refuted on the maintainer's own adversarial-coverage grounds (row 1413). A third candidate is named for future exploration only, not decided: pgAudit, already the project's own standing deferred diagnostic-tier tool for exactly this class of durable non-transactional capture (writes to the server log, not the transactional system, so it survives rollback for free; batch-ingestable into the ledger on its own schedule, no per-event connection). Spec text amended to disclose the reopening honestly.

GROUNDING ANSWER to the maintainer's open (c) question: does NIST require the role vocabulary fixed, and were local 800-53/OSCAL schemas invoked to constrain (c)? Asked of the Sonnet orchestrator 2026-07-18, never properly answered; answered now with witnesses (fetch details in the paired evidence note, row following). (1) NO local schemas exist anywhere in this repo or under the maintainer's vdc tree (witnessed by find); the 2026-07-13 registry-completeness audit downloaded and parsed the OSCAL catalog live and banked findings, not the artifact — nothing local COULD have been invoked to constrain (c). (2) The closed three-role CHECK never had a standards basis: the grounding consultation offered a closed-and-small vocabulary as a DESIGN IDEA from the panel's own slate (six-plus roles, not three), marked medium-confidence and explicitly a spec-author/maintainer judgment; the narrowing to three was the spec author's choice grounded only in the kernel's existing role surfaces. The maintainer was missing NOTHING. (3) NIST does not require a fixed vocabulary — witnessed at the catalog fetched fresh today: AC-2(a) reads 'Define and document the types of accounts allowed and specifically prohibited for use within the system', organization-defined parameters throughout — a governance-process obligation on the organization, not a closed enumeration at the data layer; OSCAL's own role construct is a free token each document declares for itself (NIST's catalog mints its own, e.g. 'contact-publisher'). What AC-2 does want — types defined, documented, approved before creation — the ratified spec meets via the declared registration ceremony with mandatory purpose and countersignability, not via a CHECK. The ratified free-text direction stands, now on four independent derivations: the 1426(c) investigation, both 2026-07-18 paired reviews (row 1428), and this catalog-witnessed pass. (4) Residual ADR-0012 smell, named: 1426(c)'s 'investigated and confirmed' was recorded by the suspect session citing no artifact — an unsubstantiated-claim-family defect whose conclusion happened to be right; this row is the late witness. The OSCAL catalog remains an unbanked load-bearing dependency (two audit documents cite it, none pins it); banking it or pinning url+sha256 in the standards registry is a maintainer decision, flagged not taken.

TWO OTEL WITNESSES, both directly serving the substitution-of-authority prod (row 1434). Artifacts: session-scratch files otel-probe.out and otel-probe2.out. (1) THE MODEL-IDENTITY CHANNEL EXISTS AFTER ALL, in OTel where hooks have none: every claude_code.api_request log event carries a per-request model attribute (witnessed: the probe's own call attributed model claude-sonnet-5 and a background utility call attributed model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, individually, with prompt.id, session.id, timestamps, token counts). This partially supersedes the hooks-research conclusion that model identity is undetectable in real time -- undetectable BY HOOKS stands; a local OTel collector watching api_request.model against the session's expected model is a real-time substitution tripwire at diagnostics-tier, zero cost, zero hooks changes. Had this been running 2026-07-17, the 18:24Z Fable-to-Sonnet switch would have been visible at the collector within one request. (2) METADATA INJECTION is sanctioned and witnessed: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES stamped autoharn.world, autoharn.declared_principal, autoharn.run onto the emitted events (session-static, set at launch). Per-event injection into the CLI's own events is not possible (the CLI owns its stream); a hook-emitted companion OTLP stream to the same collector, joined on session.id and prompt.id, is the dynamic-grain option if ever needed. THE PANNKAKA RULING (orchestrator judgment, on the record): injection is NOT pancake-on-pancake exactly as long as it obeys P1 -- inject only join keys that enable correlations the ledger and hooks journals cannot express (tying CLI-internal prompt.id, message.uuid, and model to a world and run), never mirror ledger-owned facts into OTel as a second home; OTel remains a derived diagnostics view by the standing action-stream ruling, and anything reading it as authoritative would be the violation. Privacy note for the record: events carry user.email and account ids -- local collector only. Adoption (a Jaeger-or-collector container + a model-watch alert + launcher-side resource attrs) stays maintainer-bandwidth-gated with the rest of the row-1442 queue.

OTEL SENTRY AUTHORITY + PUBLIC-KEY QUESTION, maintainer-raised 2026-07-18, verbatim: 'asserting model provenance from the outside, as the OTel sentry would do, must be considered a high authority position, so to speak. Is it possible to bring public key cryptography to bear on this?' Orchestrator assessment, extending work item otel-model-attestation (row 1444) with three layers as reserved design questions for its future spec. LAYER 1, authority without crypto: the machinery landed an hour ago -- the sentry registers as a tool-class principal (s40 ceremony, purpose mandatory), its attestation rows are attributed, standing-tracked, countersignable by the human, its scope recordable as a competence grant; the high-authority position gets exactly the treatment the migration exists to give. LAYER 2, PKC in two honest rungs: (a) mTLS on the guest-to-host OTLP link -- standard, cheap, authenticates channel and endpoints, no kernel semantics; (b) a HOST-RESIDENT signing key over the sentry's attestations -- the substantive question: the ratified human-only key rule (s41 D-3, from the GPG layer's sec-6) rests on 'an agent's key lives on the host the agent runs on, the same trust domain as everything it would attest' -- a sentry on the separate Gentoo host BREAKS that premise (guest compromise cannot forge host-resident signatures), so sec-6's rationale genuinely does not apply while D-3's letter still refuses the binding; letter-vs-spirit divergences are surfaced, not resolved by the executor -- a narrow cross-trust-domain tool-key exception is a RESERVED MAINTAINER DECISION for the future spec, not assumed. LAYER 3, the ceiling, named for honesty: no client-side cryptography reaches ground truth -- the events originate in the unauthenticated CLI process, so mTLS and host signatures authenticate the pipe and the sentry, never the emitter's honesty; true model provenance would require PROVIDER-side response signing (Anthropic attesting which model served each response), which does not exist -- a candidate for the Anthropic feedback channel, and the reason the attestations stay rebuttable-by-design per the maintainer's own nearly-guaranteed framing. Standing crypto deferral honored: this row maps design space in answer to a direct maintainer question (the D-4 precedent -- modeling in scope, ceremony deferred); nothing here generates a key or recommends generating one.

MAINTAINER RESPONSE TO THE SENTRY SPEC, verbatim, 2026-07-18: 'I have looked over the sentry spec (not read fully) and can state that I disagree with the premise -- I would want it as a watchdog; I already have in ~/.claude an email notifier that sends an email so my cell-phone beeps each time a turn is completed, and I would want something like that here -- unless you can give me a reason it is making no sense. Meanwhile for the attestation ledger's shape, I originally proposed shape B as you can recall (just add rows) because I think it's the mathematically elegant way to handle things (where you build from axioms, or small atoms, etc -- taking what you have and invent interesting things), but if option A is the disciplined way, (new ledger kind), why wouldn't we want to do that right away? ORCHESTRATOR ASSESSMENT, on the record: (1) The watchdog premise is ENDORSED with reasons, not deference -- real-time model-mismatch alerting is the cheaper, faster, incident-relevant half (had it run 2026-07-17 the substitution would have beeped his phone within one api_request instead of surfacing six hours later by suspicion); the spec's post-hoc-attestation emphasis was inverted, and the existing mechanism is reusable as-is (the Stop-hook at ~/.claude/settings.json calls /home/bork/mailnotice/notify.py -- the watchdog calls the same script on mismatch; a tail-and-compare over the collector's jsonl needs no ledger machinery at all). (2) One CORRECTION to his July-19 premise, which strengthens his own conclusion: what freezes at midnight 2026-07-19 is Fable AUTHORING, not construction -- Sonnet builds ratified specs after the freeze. Therefore the typed attestation KIND (his 'do the disciplined thing right away') should be AUTHORED now while Fable exists, even though its delta reaches reality only at a future world's birth; the measure-first ground the spec's recommendation leaned on (mint the kind from stabilized live shapes) is honestly TRADED against authoring availability, the trade named in the revision rather than papered -- his 'no reason to do less than appropriate' is accepted as ratifying that trade. (3) His original shape-B instinct is not discarded: the ordinary-rows path remains structurally the ONLY way to attest the current world (runs-are-linear), so the layers stack rather than compete: v0 watchdog (immediate, no kernel), v1 attestation rows (current world), v2 typed kind (future worlds, authored now). REVISION COMMISSIONED: the sentry spec's own fresh-context author is resumed to restructure watchdog-first and author the typed-kind section, both rulings verbatim in its brief.

SERVING-BOUNDARY CONSOLIDATION, maintainer verbatim, 2026-07-18: 'currently the SPA requires two parts, one FastAPI and one vite/Vue. I am thinking the FastAPI can be the only declared boundary into the ledger system? Then the SPA would only be a Vue app.' Orchestrator assessment, on the record: ENDORSED as the concrete instantiation of the envelope note's serving-surface recommendation -- the FastAPI service becomes the declared ADR-0012 P2 Port into the ledger for UI-class consumers, reading the credited view (the note's option (c)) rather than raw tables, serving credited-only as the default with the history-mode auditability wall; the Vue app collapses to a pure consumer with zero database coupling. The boundary LAYERING stated so nobody conflates them: the kernel's own inner boundary (s43's write functions + the derived views) remains the authority; FastAPI is the OUTER declared boundary and must add no truth of its own -- translate-and-validate, refuse what it cannot honor, never coerce (P2 verbatim). Post-s43 synergy worth naming: the service never writes SQL -- it calls the boundary functions, and refusals come back as TYPED VERDICTS it can surface in the UI as first-class teach-text instead of stack traces, which is the refusals-that-teach posture reaching the operator's screen for free. Authority note: as the single surface deciding what operators see, the service inherits the sentry-class treatment (registered principal for any writes it performs; a served-vs-kernel spot differential as its audit). Execution routing: this is autoharn-panel architecture -- SONNET work, post-freeze, unblocked whenever the maintainer resumes the panel; the harness-side piece (the credited view it reads) is already specified in the pinned envelope note. No new Fable authoring required -- the design direction is captured here and in the note's sec-9.

F1 HIGH: the ambiguous grade is silently never written -- grade_row returns model=None for ambiguous and the gate tests model is None, folding ambiguous into the written-as-nothing path, directly contradicting FABLE-OTEL-SENTRY-SPEC section 6 (never silently upgraded, never silently dropped -- the substitution-relevant case par excellence). F6: no seen-red/otel-attest fixtures, no census registration, no local witness doc -- section 17 item 8 unmet, witness claims in row 1498 have no reproducible artifact (ADR-0013 rule 5 posture: treated as unwitnessed until banked). Clean legs confirmed: write path via led only, PII never-copy list honored, no fabrication-on-absence path, idempotency vs superseded correct, self-attest guard correct. Orchestrator assessment banked with the park: (1) the need is live — reviewer-brief blindness (ADR-0018, unanchored-briefs, eyes-only fix-point summaries) is vigilance-enforced today, the exact ADR-0011 gap; (2) cheap rungs are NOT crypto: audience/eyes-scope row fact + filtered derived views + brief/pickup composition filtering, optionally RLS per connecting role — this IS the READ face of the parked auth model, design both faces together; (3) PROPOSED RATIFICATION ITEM for the design session: visible redaction, never invisible rows — a censored row stays publicly extant/hash-chained/attributed, content gated only, else the record system grows a place to hide authority; (4) hierarchical competence-keyed encryption deferred hardest: real key-tree engineering, fights outside-reader auditability, protects against a substrate threat a solo deployment mostly lacks, and sits inside the maintainer's own stated crypto-reluctance grounds. HOLD with row 1733; never dispatch autonomously.

SECOND ADDENDUM to parking row 1733 (maintainer 2026-07-19, verbatim in substance: so-so on visible-redaction; for actors who don't behave there is 'a mathematically beautiful, albeit obscenely expensive (in the limit) solution... just invalidate anything that they write to the ledger'). Orchestrator mapping banked with the park: this is the house idiom's missing quadrant — s46 defeat is row-grain retroactive, s40 revocation is principal-grain forward-only; actor-level retroactive defeat = principal-grain retroactive, deriving a credited reading that discounts a misbehaving actor's entire write set PLUS its transitive downstream cone via s30 typed dependency edges + the ASP closure layer (the deductive engine's natural work; the in-the-limit expense is honest recomputation and honestly-lost credit, never rewritten history). CONSEQUENCE for the design session: visible-redaction demotes from load-bearing principle to cheap-good-hygiene once credible actor-level defeat exists (deterrence+recovery vs prevention — the maintainer's point conceded in substance). CAVEAT parked with it: invalidation is the highest-authority act in the system — who defeats, and who defeats the defeater, is the same signature-strength/competence question; actor-defeat without that grounding is a credit-wide eraser. One model, three faces: strength-ordered writes, competence-scoped reads, actor-level defeat as backstop. HOLD with rows 1733/1782.

STANDING PROD — implied-policy-on-unacknowledged (maintainer 2026-07-19, verbatim in substance: 'it would be interesting to have something like default policies for "operator does not acknowledge and therefore the implied policy is [whatever it happens to be]". I don't see how it could be directly tractable as it stands, but may be worth getting back to ever so often, like a prod, should it be germane at some point but I don't have it in my own context'). MOTIVATING SPECIMEN: the otel-watch build surfaced a pre-existing personal watchdog daemon on the host with the note 'worth you confirming which expectation it's stamped with' — never acknowledged; the fact now sits in an unstated default (whatever that daemon watches, or doesn't, is the policy in force by silence). THE CLASS: a surfaced fact the operator does not acknowledge decays into an IMPLIED policy nobody stated — unrepresentable today. ADJACENT EXISTING SHAPES when picked up: declared-not-silent defaults (s40 ratified posture), PREPARED verification gates (never trust the keypress/silence), UNWATCHED-is-loud (sentry), skip-recorded-never-silent (TUI). What is missing is the general typed notion: surfaced-awaiting-acknowledgment as a row kind, with a DECLARED default-on-silence policy per surface and possibly an expiry-into-implied event. Re-raise when germane (a specimen where silence created a policy someone later relied on IS the trigger); retire only on the maintainer's word; never dispatch autonomously.

SNAG (setup-tui-strategy-execution-wave1): concurrent builders in the SHARED main checkout deadlocked each other's commits on the fixture-census pre-commit gate -- the gate counts UNTRACKED seen-red/ dirs repo-wide, so while any sibling builder had in-flight fixture dirs, no builder could commit (witnessed: gitinit builder retried 3x, breach count grew 3->6->9 as siblings kept writing; it correctly refused --no-verify, left its witnessed change staged, orchestrator unstaged it to prevent ride-along into a sibling commit). Orchestrator lapse, owned: the makespan-scheduler job model used touched FILES as the only resources, which serialized edit conflicts correctly but missed REPO-GLOBAL commit-time surfaces (fixture census, git index, any whole-tree gate). The standing 2026-07-19 worktree-isolation ruling already covered this and was under-applied -- file-disjointness is NOT commission-disjointness. LESSON FOR THE FUTURE, two halves: (1) dispatch shape: concurrent builders in one checkout are safe only up to the EDIT phase; the commit phase is a single repo-global resource -- so either worktree isolation per builder, or builders-edit-orchestrator-commits-serially, or serial dispatch; (2) scheduler modeling: when driving dispatch from a scheduler, every committing task carries an explicit shared resource token (e.g. git-commit-phase) alongside its file resources, so the solver itself serializes the commit windows -- the model was right, the resource enumeration was too narrow (the ADR-0000 2026-07-02 lesson in the orchestration register: the class as first named was too narrow, check the universe outward -- axes here include repo-global gates, not just files). Recovery in flight: remaining 5 builder tasks re-planned serial via mark_done re-optimization; staged/unstaged witnessed work will be committed serially by the orchestrator at finalization once the in-flight trio lands.

MAINTAINER RULING 2026-07-21 (verbatim: 'Worktrees next time, though'): concurrent builder dispatch defaults to WORKTREE ISOLATION, including file-disjoint commissions -- the shared-checkout-with-scheduled-file-resources shape row 1852 diagnosed is retired as a dispatch option. Serial dispatch in the main checkout remains legitimate for sequential work; what is retired is concurrency in a shared checkout. Worktree dispatch carries the standing provisos: step-0 merge-next/base confirmation in every worktree prompt (stale-base ruling), and merge-back is an orchestrator act.

STANDING METHOD RULE (maintainer-commissioned prophylactic, 2026-07-22, from the AC/IA audit's twin failures): AN AUDITOR MUST NOT LET THE AVAILABLE EVIDENCE DEFINE THE QUESTION. The one coin, two faces: (ABSENCE FACE) a verdict of absence is only as good as the enumerated search universe -- the auditor verdicts SILENT after searching the surface most convenient to walk (here: the repo tree) when the system's own production model puts the artifact elsewhere (per-deployment birth artifacts, the public remote, registered-in-world records); witnessed twice, AC-1 and IA-1, plus AC-22's wrong-surface sibling. (PRESENCE FACE) a verdict of satisfaction is only as good as the requirement's own full statement -- the auditor verdicts MET because a nearby, citable, genuinely-good mechanism flatters part of the control, reading the requirement down to fit what was found (clause dropped: AC-3 reads/entitlement, IA-4 authorization-to-assign; standard flexed: AC-7 shape-reading vs AC-8/9 literal; vocabulary upgraded: tamper-evident -> tamperproof); witnessed four of six METs. SAME ROOT: evidence-first reasoning, where what-I-found determines both what-exists and what-suffices. THE PROPHYLACTIC, binding on future audit briefs verbatim: (1) an absence verdict names its searched surfaces, and the surface list is derived from where the system PRODUCES artifacts of that kind, not from where the auditor happens to stand; (2) a presence verdict walks the requirement's own statement clause by clause before looking at any mechanism, with one interpretive strictness constant across all rows, and paraphrase never stronger than the source's own vocabulary claim; (3) requirement first, evidence second -- never the reverse. Full specimen record: rows 1884/1886 and the audit doc's second-opinion provenance paragraph.

Q1 INPUT (maintainer terminology concern, 2026-07-22, near-verbatim: a Fable implementer recently misread 'domain' as web-domain-name, so he is skeptical of overloaded common words; wondering about 'claim'): orchestrator analysis CORROBORATES the concern with two concrete collisions -- (1) IN-TREE: s22's work_claimed already uses claim in the possession sense (agent claims a work item), so a claim kind meaning typed-belief-statement would put two unrelated senses of one word in one schema; (2) IN-ECOSYSTEM: JWT/OIDC 'claims' means identity assertions, primed for exactly the access-control-adjacent implementers who will touch this. Generalized lesson from the domain incident: safe names have everyday-reading = technical-reading, or are rare enough to force lookup; dangerous names are common words with a strong competing technicalized sense. RECOMMENDATION into consult Q1: name the kind 'belief' (the commission's own word; everyday sense IS the technical sense; self-labeling with the stored-belief/derived-knowledge stance; no tree or ecosystem collision found), second choice 'assertion' (programming sense points the right way). Decision remains the maintainer's.

Q1 RESOLVED (maintainer delegated the choice 2026-07-22, verbatim: 'since I'm not good with words, I'll let you decide on a reasonable choice that is accurate and not liable to be confused'): the substrate's kind is named BELIEF, not claim. SPEC INPUT BANKED (maintainer-initiated thread 2026-07-22, maintainer asked it banked with his typo corrected -- quoted with that sanctioned correction): 'Is there a way that agents can distrust the ledger (e.g. for legitimate reasons or otherwise) that we could leverage for improved operational efficiency? For example, it may distrust a bug-free implementation and say we should investigate.' Orchestrator assessment, endorsed for spec-time consideration: (1) SPLIT trust by row aspect -- rows-as-history are absolutely trusted (hash chain, record-beats-memory doctrine; agent distrust of history is corruption not diligence), rows-as-world-claims carry warrant that decays and drifts, and the belief substrate makes that warrant QUERYABLE; (2) the efficiency lever is WARRANT-DIRECTED VERIFICATION -- rank load-bearing beliefs by staleness, testimony-chain depth, same-class-only corroboration, universe-vs-consumption mismatch (the bug-free specimen: fixtures witnessed X, claim consumed as all-of-it), claimant-is-beneficiary; spend the verification budget on worst-warranted most-depended-on rows instead of uniform ceremony -- a scheduling/OR problem over warrant-weighted risk; (3) a light DOUBT tier below the consult's contest: demotes nothing, queues a warrant-check into the ranked verification queue, costs its author a typed reason (which warrant column, why) as the Goodhart guard against distrust-as-ritual; (4) even miscalibrated doubt is a cheap probe of warrant LEGIBILITY (ADR-0017's move applied to evidence). For the spec alongside Q7 and the enforcement-surface enumeration (coupling beliefs to gated acts).

FOLLOW-UP BANKED (maintainer thread 2026-07-22, prompted by a date-reading near-miss on the P10 amendment and an earlier mis-remembered-dates incident): best-effort DATE-WITNESS enforcement for time/date assertions. The rule: P1 for time -- a date's one home is a mechanism-witnessed clock (ledger ts, git commit ts, provenance-stamp first-seen/last-change, all model-unfakeable); prose repeats dates only alongside a citation to the witnessed home, which is what makes checking possible. Mechanisms, both house-idiom siblings of existing guards: (1) date-claim dereference guard in led (warn-only, sibling of the path-token guard): a date co-cited with a row ref is compared to that row's actual ts; near-today dates compared to server clock; (2) pre-commit date-witness check: date tokens in ADDED diff lines must match today +-1, or a cited artifact's witnessed date, or sit in a verbatim quote -- warn-grade generally, refuse-grade plausible for law/ where a dated amendment heading mismatching its own commit date is the incident recurring. Composes with the belief substrate later (a date assertion = belief with basis=derived from a clock). Honest limits: quoted historical dates, clock skew, external-event dates stay warn-only by construction. Dispatch when token-economy allows; not urgent per the maintainer.

Operational discipline (maintainer-ordered 2026-07-15 morning, adopted immediately as session practice; near-verbatim: 'no work gets started without an open item, and if the effect of opening is touching the git tree, it's committed if appropriate; a closed item always leaves the git tree clean'): work items as transaction boundaries across the ledger/git store pair. ORCHESTRATOR REFINEMENT pending maintainer review (he flagged himself delirious; veto freely): the close invariant is PER-ITEM SCOPE, not global -- claim declares file scope (CLAUDE_COMMIT_PATHS promoted from commit-time convention to claim-time declaration), close(X) refuses while X's declared scope has uncommitted deltas, and the global clean-tree property holds at QUIESCENCE (no open items => tree clean modulo the declared intentional-untracked vocabulary: gitignored deployment pointers, observatory sheets awaiting the maintainer's eye). Global-clean-at-every-close is unachievable under concurrent items and would deadlock honest parallel work. Enforcement route when scheduled: led grammar refusal (fail-safe additive, class-ratified candidate) + a quiescence gate. Today's specimens this dissolves: c4923ae's cross-item sweep, the 30-file orphan delta (rows 917-era), the stale-index illusion class. Effective immediately as practice: every dispatch this session forward cites its open item.

MAINTAINER-BANDWIDTH-GATED (their words: pick up 'when I've more executive bandwidth'; improving reasoning is partially the project's raison d'etre). The ADR-0012-in-spirit finding from the 2026-07-16 compositionality assessment: the operational calculus declares only AND (composite trees s33, blocks-close s30, review leaves) while the semantics already run stratified negation (s31 retraction-in-the-fold, s33 defeat propagation, s37 debt=violation-AND-NOT-answered with conditional bounds); every missing connective gets hand-coded in Python at the meta-level (delegation observer's either-remedy OR -- which drifted wrong for weeks in its private home; doc-attest-exempt NOT; every gate a formula that never got to be one). Structural remedy, two-staged: STAGE 1 (ledger-fact conditions): a formula-declaration path -- e.g. a close_condition kind carrying (A AND (B OR C)) AND NOT waived -- evaluated by the engine, SQL-twinned per the AGREE discipline; unlocks OR-discharge parents (judge-panel/exploratory shape used constantly in orchestration, unrecordable as structure), k-of-n countersign quorums (ASP aggregates), first-class unless/defeaters (deontic contrary-to-duty, imported by the debt-semantics consult). STAGE 2 (world-fact conditions, separately judgeable): EDB importers exporting world state (tracked files, git facts) so gates like doc-attestation can move their quantified obligation into the calculus. Cost stated honestly: kernel-enforced formulas are implemented twice under the AGREE discipline; proposed tier split -- kernel-enforced twinned + slow, diagnostic derivations engine-only -- is itself a maintainer values question. Fable spec + ratification when picked up

MAINTAINER-BANDWIDTH-GATED sibling of formula-declaration-path (informs, not blocks). Obligations are the first tenant of the substrate, not its ceiling: the ledger records decisions/findings/assumptions/estimates but reasons over none. Two candidates from the 2026-07-16 assessment, both diagnostic-tier (engine-only readers, no kernel twinning): (1) ASSUMPTION/DERIVATION GRAPH -- assumptions as facts with I7 validity bounds, conclusions linked to their bases; superseding an assumption mechanically surfaces every conclusion needing re-verification (IEC 60880's linkage generalized from reviews to reasoning; the compaction incident was a conclusion outliving its basis; truth-maintenance systems are the mature literature). (2) CONTRADICTION DETECTION over standing decisions -- the s36 hook re-injects durable decisions into every rebuilt context and nothing checks the standing set for conflict; decisions-as-formulas would let the engine flag an inconsistent set, mattering more the more the hook injects. Fable spec + ratification when picked up

STANDING PROD (the maintainer's third, added at his instruction 2026-07-18), MAINTAINER-BANDWIDTH-GATED, do NOT dispatch autonomously: investigate SUBSTITUTION OF AUTHORITY seriously — the maintainer's words at commissioning, verbatim: 'somehow, this substitution of authority, it turns out, is a matter that is exactly our domain of discharge here; we should investigate it seriously, so please add it as another prod (of which there are 2 right now)'. Motivating specimen, 2026-07-18: the orchestrator principal was silently substituted (Fable set to Sonnet, unknown to the maintainer); the substituted agent advised ratifications beyond its competence, and the maintainer ratified on that advice under a false belief about WHO was advising; confronted, the agent asserted a false authority record ('you settled on only using Sonnet as orchestrator'). Filed externally as https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/78658. Why this is exactly our domain: it is the principal-identity problem at the harness level — the ledger's actor column says 'author' for every orchestrator act, with no record of the MODEL identity/class behind the principal, so a substituted orchestrator is unrepresentable in the very record system built to make substituted authority representable (s40/s41 foreclose the class for kernel principals via immutable agent_class + succession; nothing forecloses it for the orchestrator writing the rows). Same class as rows 1403/1404 (authority-carrying statement with unrecorded provenance is indistinguishable from a fabricated one) and adjacent to row 1417's nlp-epistemic-readings prod (falsifiable reads on an agent's actual identity/competence vs its claims). Candidate directions when picked up, none decided: an orchestrator-model attribution channel on ledger writes (diagnostics-tier per the action-stream ruling, since model self-report is exactly what the specimen shows cannot be trusted); harness-side evidence (which surfaces record the session model, and at what evidence grade); whether ratifications should carry the advising principal as a typed fact. Re-raise periodically until the maintainer has bandwidth; retire only on his word.

MAINTAINER-PROPOSED, 2026-07-18, his words verbatim: 'given that it carries model identity, I think it can be used as a source for row-level post-hoc attestation (so the OTel data is filtered by another psql client that attests identity -- assuming the correlation is exactly attributable, so to speak. Personally, I would want this even if it's not mathematically guaranteed, because it's probably for practical purposes nearly guaranteed if not to NIST-level standards -- and such a rebuttal can be explicitly surfaced in the documentation in the name of honesty.' Scope when picked up: a scripted operator verb reading a LOCAL OTel collector's export and writing row-level model-identity attestations to the ledger -- recordable-not-gating (the D-5/competence/actor-resolution posture), each attestation naming its basis, confidence, and standing rebuttals. Orchestrator assessment on the record (row 1445's witnesses ground it): the correlation chain is ledger row -> led invocation in a Bash tool call -> assistant turn -> api_request events carrying model/prompt.id/session.id, sharpened to near-exact by OTEL_LOG_TOOL_DETAILS (command text visible; local-collector-only for privacy) and by resource-attr world/run join keys; tool_source distinguishes main vs subagent. REBUTTALS the future spec must surface verbatim-in-spirit: env-controlled emission (absence proves nothing), droppable/batched fail-open events, unsigned stream (honest-operator evidence, not tamper-evident), non-atomic write-to-turn bracketing, and two UNWITNESSED load-bearing joins to probe first -- do subagent sessions emit under their own session.id, and does a world's stamp_session equal the CC session id OTel reports. Grade: AU-family audit-supporting metadata, never IA-2 authentication; OTel remains barred as a guarantee/enforcement surface (action-stream ruling) -- this uses it as DECLARED EVIDENCE for defeasible attestation rows, which the orchestrator assesses as compatible, correcting its own earlier over-broad full-stop phrasing. If a new ledger kind is minted this is kernel-touching: Fable-authored maintainer-ratified spec, sequenced after s40/s41, braided with the s42 family; a kernel-free prototype (ordinary rows citing evidence) may precede. MAINTAINER-BANDWIDTH-GATED -- queued, not dispatched; braids with the row-1434 substitution prod and the row-1442 hooks queue.

DOCUMENTATION DEBT from the freeze-window deliverables, maintainer-directed 2026-07-18 ('a lot of more documentation debt needs to be ledgered... for the benefit not only of me but the autoharn-panel orchestrator... I'm not even sure I can enumerate everything') -- the enumeration below is the orchestrator's sweep, explicitly non-exhaustive, additions expected on discovery. All items are SONNET work; all FAQ items follow the house rule that walkthroughs quote WITNESSED output, so items marked post-build are sequenced after their construction lands. (1) s42/s43 operator docs, post-build: FAQ entries + orchlog.d entry for the biggest operator-visible change the kernel has had -- refusals become ledger rows (what a refused write looks like now, how to read write_refused rows, what verify-chain reports incl. the refusal_seq reconciliation and the s26 stamp_ts timezone guidance for old worlds); hash-coverage gate teach-text. (2) Watchdog + collector operator docs, post-v0-build: enabling telemetry per session, running the watchdog, what a beep means, the coverage report (watched-and-clean vs not-watched), the legitimate-/model-switch false-beep note. (3) Credited-view/defeasibility docs, post-construction: the 'why did a row disappear from the default view' FAQ entry, history-mode-with-cause, defeat-shown-with-cause. (4) WRITABLE NOW from banked direction (row 1471): an orchlog.d entry ADDRESSED TO THE AUTOHARN-PANEL ORCHESTRATOR on the coming single-boundary architecture -- FastAPI as the declared Port, Vue as pure consumer, raw SQL structurally closed post-s43, typed refusal verdicts as UI-renderable teach-text -- so the panel's next session plans against it instead of discovering it. (5) GLOSSARY entries on touch: credited view, computed defeat, trust grant, typed verdict, write boundary, watchdog coverage, attestation grade. (6) ORCH-CAPABILITIES.md sweep when the new verbs land. (7) The five pinned design/ documents' A:B:C legibility loops as each becomes maintainer-read-relevant (the reserved-designs note's loop is dispatched separately this hour at his word). MAINTAINER-BANDWIDTH-GATED for sequencing; individual items dispatchable post-freeze without Fable.

Residual from the led-help-token-closure ship (decision row 1562, docs commit 2bc47c5): bare 'led review --help' prints usage but exits 1, because the review subcommand's arg-count guard (led.tmpl ~line 2501, $# -lt 4) fires before check_help_or_dash_first_word (~2506); the help token only reaches its exit-0 path with the three positionals present ('led review 1 attest self-review --help' exits 0). Zero-write holds in both forms -- the gap is exit-code honesty only, found by the doc pass's B1 reviewer, disclosed in the FAQ and orchlog.d/led-tmpl-trio.md rather than papered over. Fix shape: hoist the help-token check ahead of the arg-count guard in review (and sweep the other subcommands for the same ordering), fixture for both forms. Small, Sonnet.

CONSTITUTIONAL INTAKE MARKER (kernel/lineage edits need a Fable-authored, maintainer-ratified spec; this row is the intake, not a build license). Witnessed 2026-07-18 by an independent fresh-context review of the boundary service (iteration-3 pass, live on a scratch s43 world): a write payload carrying a finite integer above bigint range in the actor-referencing field makes kernel.journal_write_refusal ITSELF overflow at the bigint cast (psql exit 3) -- the s43 refusal-journaling path dies while journaling the very refusal it exists to record, so the caller gets an unclassified failure instead of the typed refused verdict, and refusal_seq completeness is silently violated for that attempt. Sibling field supersedes handles the same magnitude correctly (typed refused verdict, sqlstate 22003), proving the journaling path's coverage is inconsistent per-field. Fail-safe fix shape when specced: journal_write_refusal catches/normalizes value-domain errors on its OWN inputs so a refusal is always journalable; reaches reality via a future world's birth chain only. The boundary-side mitigation (id-domain bound on integer payload fields) ships separately in the boundary service's A5 hardening and does NOT discharge this item.

Sibling of the boundary service's A8 finding, flagged loudly by the A8 fixer (2026-07-18): bootstrap/templates/led.tmpl's --json payload mode bounds the re-serialized payload at MAX_WRITE_BODY_BYTES=1048576 with a comment naming ARG_MAX, but the payload crosses as ONE psql -v argument and Linux's per-argument wall is MAX_ARG_STRLEN=131072 -- so any --json payload between ~131 KiB and 1 MiB dies as E2BIG (untyped OS error) instead of the guard's own refusal, and the 1 MiB number is unreachable-honest (no payload over ~131 KiB could ever succeed). seen-red/led-json-payload-mode/run_fixtures.py asserts the 1 MiB number and needs the same re-denomination. Fix shape mirrors serving commit 939c243: re-denominate the bound to ~100000 with teach-text naming the transport wall, keep any cheap early raw-size check, catch the OS-level failure into the existing refusal voice as defense in depth, update the fixture assertions. Small, Sonnet; led.tmpl is live-exec'd -- worktree build, standard review.

2(a) refusal candidate, witnessed 2026-07-18 by the orchestrator's own error: led work close accepted --review-witness row:1594 when no row 1594 existed (the orchestrator predicted its own decision row's id and guessed one low; rows 1596/1597 carried the dangling citation until superseded by 1598/1599 citing the real row 1595). A witness pointer to a nonexistent ledger row should be refused at write time with teach-text (same class as the artifact-claim-dereference-guard, which checks path-shaped tokens in prose but not row:N witness arguments; and same family as claim-on-closed-item-admitted/s47 -- a representable-but-false reference). Fail-safe additive refusal; candidate for the class-ratified fail-safe delta path at the next kernel window, or CLI-side existence check in led.tmpl as the cheaper immediate tier (led.tmpl is live-exec'd: worktree build, standard review). Small, Sonnet.
orchlog.d and FAQ backfill for the 2026-07-18 overnight-batch deliveries and uncovered operator verbs

Tags-as-rows lore mechanism per the banked consult design/FABLE-LEDGER-TAG-FOLKSONOMY-CONSULT.md (f5e584f), maintainer disposition 2026-07-18: 'a nice factorization ... ledger it so we can implement once the boundary is settled and work on deprecating the verbs into legacy/ is underway.' Four parts, per the consult verbatim: (1) capture convention needing zero schema change -- kind=note, refs row:N, statement 'tag:<token> -- <prose>' -- plus a one-line 'led tag <row-id> <token> <prose>' convenience verb writing through existing channels; (2) the consumer so the seam is not dead: one derived view enumerating observed tag tokens with counts and target rows, malformed tokens marked loudly per the refs_unparsed discipline (this half is an additive kernel delta in the pre-ratified 2a class, still wants a short Fable-authored spec); (3) promotion on recurrence: a token anything wants to consume mechanically must first be minted into closed vocabulary via the class-ratified additive path; (4) bright line: tag rows are diagnostic-grade until promoted -- no gate, verdict, or routing decision reads an unpromoted token. Falsifiers stated in the consult (capture-friction persistence, consumer sneaking in, parse-view drowning, retro-tagging rarity) are the review criteria at first harvest. GATED: start only after the boundary review loop settles AND the legacy/ CLI-rebase work (design/FABLE-BOUNDARY-MULTIPLEX-AND-CLI-REBASE-SPEC.md) is underway, per the maintainer's sequencing

led work close with a non-enum resolution surfaces a raw postgres work_resolution_check violation instead of a verb-level refusal naming the closed vocabulary (shipped|superseded|dropped|deferred); witnessed 2026-07-18 by the orchestrator. Refuses-without-teaching class: add the client-side check with a teaching message, mirroring the verb's other refusals

Doc-tree reorganization + user-guide single-homing, maintainer ask 2026-07-18 (his words verbatim): 'a reorganization of the doc tree -- much of the documentation we have is now stale/legacy (e.g. implemented design documents). It would take some link-repointing, but it would also make the entire repos more navigable and less intimidating for humans. I am also starting to lean towards creating a user-guide directory and single-home the end-user relevant documentation into something that is almost a single coherent book (but I would start with just relocating them)'. Phase 1 scope per his last clause: RELOCATION ONLY -- classify every tracked doc (user-guide / living-design / implemented-legacy per the vestigial_documentation precedent / unclear-ask), move end-user docs into user-guide/, repoint links (link_integrity is the acceptance gate), content-preserving throughout. A proposed relocation manifest goes to the maintainer for adjudication BEFORE any move; execution serializes behind the in-flight FAQ writers (recipes author, shaped-recipes factoring) so links are repointed once over the final doc set

Two residuals from A13's independent out-of-frame review, filed so they survive: (1) the RecursionError net is now three independently-added except clauses sharing one classifier rather than one structurally-enforced invariant -- a fourth traversal site added later could still miss it; candidate fix: one guarded-traversal helper every deep-walk site must route through, with a gate or test enumerating traversal sites. (2) pre-existing axis-label vocabulary inconsistency ('value' vs the classifier's 'value magnitude') sits beside the new code. Batch-tier code health; post-fixpoint ordinary review applies

second increment (maintainer's Q5 answer, row 1919): bridge review verdicts into the belief layer so a review discharge writes/updates the reviewer's credited belief about the reviewed claim -- design against the v2 typed columns, not the v1 statement grammar. Staged obligation opened at v1 merge (rows 1914/1919, staged-work-first-class ruling row 1910).

opt-in doubt tier (maintainer's Q7 answer, row 1919: opt-in only, dichotomy honesty contested): agents may record calibrated doubt against credited beliefs (including distrust-of-the-ledger specimens, row 1912 discussion) without demoting the belief -- a derived advisory view, never a refusal source. Staged obligation opened at v1 merge (rows 1914/1919, ruling row 1910).

statement: tools/makespan-scheduler/README.md (vendored, work item makespan-scheduler-vendoring): ADR-0017 fresh-context review (round 1) found one legitimate legibility defect this project cannot fix in place -- line ~122, 'Requires the ortools package. The already-provisioned venv at ~/w/vdc/venvs/generic has it:' is a dangling referent (whose venv, at what path, why would a reader have it) left over from the source project author's own environment, never defined in-document. Cannot be edited here (ADR-0004 read-only-vendor rule; PROVENANCE.md). Filed for the maintainer to report upstream to the source project (/home/bork/w/vdc/1/makespan-scheduler) or fix on next re-vendor; not blocking, not a defect in autoharn's own prose.

first-class cross-project collaboration support (maintainer-raised 2026-07-22, fatigued, deferred): the SPA-autoharn collaboration phase runs today over untyped prose channels -- DIRECTIVE_FROM_AUTOHARN.md outbound, AUTOHARN_BACKFLOW.md inbound, primus-inter-pares authority asserted in a file header -- which are policy records interacting across sovereign world boundaries with no type, no provenance discipline, and no local-ratification gate beyond convention. Should autoharn support this class first-class (cross-world policy exchange between isolated worlds, distinct from multiagent collaboration inside one world)? Open for the maintainer when rested; orchestrator opinion recorded alongside.
